title: sciadiara ruber
side_effects: Sciadiara ruber can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and swelling, particularly when ingested or applied topically without proper preparation. Additionally, it can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Improper use of the plant can also lead to respiratory problems, including coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath, especially in individuals with pre-existing respiratory conditions. Furthermore, it can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, resulting in potentially life-threatening complications.
